channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Tinubu signs executive order on virtual assets, establishes council to harmonise crypto regulation
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu signs executive order establishing a Virtual Asset Council to harmonise crypto regulation in Nigeria, closing gaps and protecting citizens
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-signs-executive-order-on-virtual-assets-establishes-council-to-harmonise-crypto-regulation/

item.title: Yayi, Adebutu supporters take campaign to Daniel’s mother-in-law’s burial
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Supporters of Senator Solomon Adeola (Yayi) and Ladi Adebutu clashed at a funeral in Abeokuta, highlighting rising Ogun politics tensions ahead of 2027.
Read More: https://punchng.com/yayi-adebutu-supporters-take-campaign-to-daniels-mother-in-laws-burial/

item.title: Szoboszlai signs new long-term Liverpool deal
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Dominik Szoboszlai has signed a new long-term deal at Liverpool.

item.title: I Campaigned For Obi, He Never Appreciated Me, Says Isaac Fayose
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Businessman and social commentator, Isaac Fayose, has expressed disappointment over what he described as the failure of Labour Party’s 2023 presidential candidate, Peter Obi, to acknowledge or appreciate his support during the last general election.   Fayose made the remarks

item.title: Mshel Hutu Exclusive changes Abuja’s real estate landscape following landmark amenities commissioning
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The conversation around luxury and resort real estate in Abuja took a dynamic turn on Saturday, 4th July 2026, as Mshel Homes officially commissioned the landmark amenities and infrastructure at Mshel Hutu Exclusive.The event attracted dignitaries, investors, captains of industry, business leaders,

item.title: Nigeria Fintech forum 2026 to hold July 30 in Lagos
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Nosh has been named platinum sponsor for the fifth edition of Nigeria Fintech Forum 2026, scheduled for July 30 at the Civic Centre in Victoria Island, Lagos. The post Nigeria Fintech forum 2026 to hold July 30 in Lagos appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Oborevwori Unveils Digital Advertising Platform To Boost Delta Revenue
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Delta State Governor, Rt. Hon. Sheriff Oborevwori has unveiled a centralised Smart Advertising Management Platform for the State Signage and Advertisement Agency (DESSAA), aimed at improving transparency, accountability and efficiency in the regulation of outdoor advertising across the state. The go

item.title: FX Market Now Liquid Enough For Minimal CBN Intervention – Cardoso
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Governor of the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), Mr Olayemi Cardoso, has said Nigeria’s foreign exchange market has attained sufficient liquidity to operate with only minimal intervention from the apex bank, describing the development as one of the key outcomes of the monetary and exchange rate

item.title: Tuchel Faces Fresh Backlash After England’s World Cup Semi-Final Collapse
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel has come under renewed criticism following the Three Lions’ dramatic 2-1 defeat to Argentina in the semi-finals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. England looked on course for a place in the final after taking the lead, only for the reigning world champions to produce a stirri

item.title: Supreme Court Orders Final Forfeiture Of Emefiele’s Luxury Properties
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The apex court’s decision effectively restores the final forfeiture of the assets, bringing to an end the legal challenge mounted by Emefiele against the Federal High Court’s order. The post Supreme Court Orders Final Forfeiture Of Emefiele’s Luxury Properties appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: JUST-IN: Tinubu Signs Executive Order To Regulate Virtual Assets, Establishes Inter-Agency Council
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has signed the Presidential Executive Order on Virtual Assets Coordination, 2026, establishing a new regulatory framework aimed at harmonising the oversight of virtual assets and strengthening governance of Nigeria’s fast-growing digital economy. The Executive Order, which take

item.title: How FX Reacts to CPI Surprises (And How to Prepare)
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: What people often miss on CPI day is that the number itself isn’t what moves the market. The common reaction is to check whether the 3.4% number is high or low. But it’s all priced in advance. The real driver of EUR/USD is the gap between the actual number and what the market was positio

item.title: Bundesliga Club Table Bid For Onyedika
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Bundesliga outfit Eintracht Frankfurt have submitted an official bid to Club Brugge for Nigerian midfielder Raphael Onyedika, Completesports.com reports. Onyedika has entered the final year of his contract with the Belgian Pro League champions, and is understood to have declined the club’s off

item.title: World Cup: Argentina baffled by Thomas Tuchel’s decision during 2-1 over England
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina were baffled by Thomas Tuchel’s strategic decisions during their 2-1 win over England in the 2026 semi-final match on Wednesday, as reported by The Independent. The publication stated that the reigning champions were taken aback by the Three Lions manager’s decision to adopt a

item.title: Troops rescue nine kidnap victims in Katsina, arrest suspected terrorist, recover 56 rustled livestock
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Kingsley Omonobi Troops of Operation Fansan Yamma have rescued nine kidnapped victims, recovered 56 rustled livestock and arrested a suspected terrorist in separate intelligence-led operations across Katsina State. The Media Information Officer of the operation, Lieutenant Colonel Aliyu Danja, di

item.title: Official: PSG announce signing of 16-year-old Nigerian midfielder
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: French Première Ligue giants Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) have announced the signing of Lagos-born Nigerian midfielder Oluchi Basil, Soccernet.ng reports. ​Oluchi, a sensational attacking midfielder, has signed her first professional contract with the Rouge et Bleu. item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A woman identified as Erdoo Nancy from Ankyoor community in Gwer East Local Government Area of Benue State has been arrested over the alleged killing of her co-wife’s child. item.title: Rescued Kogi school victims receiving care — Police
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Four Kogi school victims abducted during NECO exams have been rescued and are receiving care, police confirm. Investigation into the incident is ongoing.

item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Mortgage rates edged up to the highest level in a year, delivering a fresh blow to the housing market.

item.title: Stop hitting women — Nollywood veteran RMD joins fight against gender based violence
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Famous Nigerian actor, Richard Mofe-Damijo, popularly known as RMD, has joined in the fight against gender-based violence in Nigeria. He took up the fight after emerging as a key stakeholder in the Male Feminists Network on Friday. In his remarks in Abuja, the 65-year-old veteran actor, called for m

item.title: Tinubu mourns Amaechi’s mother, says her life was marked by selfless service
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: In a statement of condolence signed by his spokesperson, Bayo Onanuga, on Friday President Tinubu acknowledged the deceased's commitment to the Catholic faith, her exemplary devotion to family values, and her dedication to raising children of service.

item.title: Nigeria losing coastal communities to atlantic ocean – Environmental Activist raises alarm
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Environmental activist, Nnimo Bassey has called attention to the plight of coastal communities in Nigeria, warning that some have lost significant portions of their land to the Atlantic Ocean due to rising sea levels and coastal erosion. Advocating for stronger regional solidarity across West Africa

item.description: Existing-home prices just hit a new record high — but there are plenty of newly built homes selling at a discount, as desperate builders cut prices.

item.title: Dollar Hedging Costs Sink to Their Lowest Level This Year
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The cost of hedging against swings in the dollar has fallen to its lowest level this year, signaling that traders see little chance of a major catalyst disrupting the world’s reserve currency despite an uncertain Federal Reserve outlook and resurgent conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Radda Enlists Clerics, Traditional Rulers In Anti-Banditry Drive
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The governor warned that security agencies remain vigilant over emerging threats in parts of Musawa and Matazu Local Government Areas. The post Radda Enlists Clerics, Traditional Rulers In Anti-Banditry Drive appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Transparency Will Drive Mining Sector Reforms, Says Alake After Receiving ARCON Fellowship
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Minister of Solid Minerals Development, Dele Alake, has said transparent communication remains critical to achieving sustainable reforms in Nigeria’s mining sector. The post Transparency Will Drive Mining Sector Reforms, Says Alake After Receiving ARCON Fellowship appeared first on Trending News.

item.title: Tinubu Cracks Down On Crypto Fraud, Sets Up Virtual Assets Council
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu has signed the Presidential Executive Order on Virtual Assets Coordination, 2026, to harmonise the regulation of virtual assets, strengthen cooperation among the nation’s financial, revenue and capital markets agencies, protect citizens from fraud, and safeguard the

item.title: UNIMED VC urges OAU students to pursue excellence with character and service
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Vice-Chancellor, University of Medical Sciences (UNIMED), Ondo, Prof. Ebunoluwa Adejuyigbe has charged the outstanding students of Obafemi Awolowo University (OAU), Ile-Ife to embrace excellence as a lifelong journey anchored on discipline, integrity, humility and service to humanity. The post U

item.title: A Road to Lisp: Which Lisp
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://scotto.me/blog/2026-07-17-which-lisp/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48947455 Points: 3 # Comments: 0

item.title: BASF Seeks Banks to Lead IPO of €20 Billion Agrichemical Unit
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: BASF SE has invited banks to pitch for roles on an initial public offering of its agrichemical division in Frankfurt, in what could be one of the largest offerings the bourse has seen in recent years.

item.title: Family of detained US scientist breaks silence after quiet diplomacy stalls
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: On this edition of Access Asia, we speak to a former FBI official helping Youlin Chen's family. The 54-year-old American scientist has been detained on espionage charges in China since November 2024, when he was visiting relatives in Beijing. His family has chosen to speak out after quiet diplomacy

item.title: Bad week for the ‘ants’ as ripple effects of Korean crash spread out across Asia
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: It was a bad week for South Korea’s army of retail traders — or, colloquially, ants — as 1.2 million of them were clobbered with margin calls. That’s more than 3% of the country’s adult population.
